title: Drop -api suffix from operator-managed sub-resources
description:
# GitHub Issue #285: Drop -api suffix from operator-managed sub-resources

**Category**: enhancement | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: Remove the `-api` suffix from every Keystone sub-resource the operator creates so that the cluster-local DNS name of the Keystone Service — and therefore the URL seeded into the OpenStack service catalog — drops from `keystone-api.<ns>.svc.cluster.local` to plain `keystone.<ns>.svc.cluster.local`. Today `openstack endpoint list` on a freshly-bootstrapped cluster shows an asymmetric pair of hostnames:

```
| internal | http://keystone-api.openstack.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3 |
| admin    | http://keystone-api.openstack.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3 |
| public   | https://keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io:8443/v3               |
```

The `public` row already uses the bare `keystone` label thanks to CC-0088 (`spec.gateway.hostname` → `https://keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io/v3`). The internal/admin rows still carry the `-api` suffix because the bootstrap Job hardcodes that pattern in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go:67-68` (`bootstrapServiceURL()` → `fmt.Sprintf("http://%s-api.%s.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3", keystone.Name, keystone.Namespace)`), and because the underlying Service itself is named via the centralised helper `apiResourceName()` at `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_hpa.go:23-28` which appends `-api` to `keystone.Name`. The same helper drives the names of every sub-resource the operator owns: Deployment (`reconcile_deployment.go:152`), Service (`reconcile_deployment.go:453`), PodDisruptionBudget (`reconcile_deployment.go:281`), HPA (`reconcile_hpa.go:36,81`), NetworkPolicy (`reconcile_networkpolicy.go:43,150`), HTTPRoute (`reconcile_httproute.go:147,225,249`), and the `internalAPIURL()` helper (`reconcile_httproute.go:97`) which feeds the HTTPRoute backendRef. The `-api` suffix is redundant: the operator is single-purpose for Keystone, the Kubernetes resource kind already disambiguates `Deployment/keystone` from `Service/keystone`, and the suffix actively diverges from the public-facing hostname customers and operators interact with.

Concrete steps:

1. Change `apiResourceName()` in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_hpa.go:26-28` to return `keystone.Name` directly. Optionally rename the helper to something neutral (`coreResourceName` or `subResourceName`) since the `api` framing was only meaningful while the suffix existed; keep the rename mechanical.
2. Refactor `bootstrapServiceURL()` in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go:67-68` to delegate to `apiResourceName(keystone)` instead of re-implementing the `%s-api` pattern. After this, the bootstrap URL automatically tracks any future change to the helper — divergence is impossible by construction.
3. Update the two remaining hardcoded literals in the Deployment template at `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:171,178` (the container name and the named container port both spelled `"keystone-api"`). Change them to `"keystone"`. The named port is referenced through Service `targetPort` (which uses an int, not a name, see `reconcile_deployment.go:461`) and through the HTTPRoute `backendRef` port (an int, see `reconcile_httproute.go:236-238`), so this is a local cosmetic rename — no cross-resource selector needs updating.
4. Walk the tests under `operators/keystone/internal/controller/` and update assertions that currently expect `*-api.<ns>.svc.cluster.local`, `test-keystone-api`, `keystone-api` container/port names, etc. The known-affected files are `reconcile_deployment_test.go`, `reconcile_healthcheck_test.go`, `reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go`, `reconcile_hpa_test.go`, `reconcile_httproute_test.go`, `reconcile_bootstrap_test.go`, `keystone_controller_test.go`, `integration_test.go`. Roughly 100+ assertion sites in total.
5. Walk the e2e fixtures and update container-name selectors and PodChaos pod-name selectors:
   - `tests/e2e/keystone/resources/chainsaw-test.yaml` (container name assertions at `:49`, `:98`)
   - `tests/e2e/keystone/uwsgi/chainsaw-test.yaml` (container name assertions at `:49`, `:106`)
   - `tests/e2e/keystone/graceful-shutdown/chainsaw-test.yaml:73`
   - `tests/e2e/keystone/configmap-no-secrets/chainsaw-test.yaml:166`
   - `tests/e2e-chaos/api-pod-kill-pdb/chainsaw-test.yaml` and `tests/e2e-chaos/api-pod-kill-pdb/01-podchaos.yaml` (PodChaos `name: kill-keystone-api` and label-selector references — these target Pods by labelSelector, not by name, so verify the actual selectors after the rename)
6. Walk the docs and architecture references and rewrite cluster-internal hostname examples from `keystone-api.openstack.svc.cluster.local` to `keystone.openstack.svc.cluster.local`. The Quick Start has at least three sites that explicitly print the internal URL (`docs/quick-start.md:638,699` and the Fallback subsection at `:736-740`), the architecture docs under `architecture/docs/09-implementation/` and `architecture/docs/05-deployment/` reference the `keystone-api` container name and Service in code examples, and `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md`, `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md`, `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md`, `docs/guides/observability.md`, `docs/guides/advanced-configuration.md`, `docs/guides/day-2-operations.md`, and `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md` all contain matching strings. About 70+ doc sites in total. The Fallback `kubectl port-forward svc/keystone-api ...` invocation in `docs/quick-start.md:736-740` and the matching unit doc test in `tests/unit/docs/quick_start_access_section_test.sh` need to be updated together so the Fallback section keeps passing its assertion gate.
7. Update the migration-relevant comments — the apologetic `// containerResources() for the pattern used by the keystone-api container.` strings scattered across `reconcile_policyvalidation.go:168`, `reconcile_fernet.go:317`, `reconcile_credential.go:322`, `reconcile_database.go:620`, and `reconcile_bootstrap.go:134` should be touched up to drop the `-api` framing so future readers don't get confused about why a bare `keystone` container is being described as `keystone-api`.
8. Document the breaking change in the Keystone CRD reference and in the upgrade-flow reference — existing OpenStack control planes have `http://keystone-api.<ns>.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3` baked into the `endpoint` rows of the Keystone DB. After the rename, the Service `keystone-api` no longer exists, so any other OpenStack service whose `keystone_authtoken.www_authenticate_uri` / `auth_url` points at the old hostname will fail. The operator's bootstrap reconciler runs `keystone-manage bootstrap` which is idempotent and updates the existing admin/internal/public endpoint rows in-place; on the next reconcile after the upgrade, the catalog will self-heal to the new hostname. Document this explicitly in the upgrade-flow guide so operators know to either: (a) re-roll the bootstrap Job (the operator's BootstrapReady condition flips to False on Generation change, which forces re-bootstrap), or (b) run `openstack endpoint set` against the two cluster-internal rows after the upgrade. Add a one-line callout to the CRD reference explaining that the operator-managed Service is now named after the CR (`metadata.name`) without suffix.

**Motivation**: Three concrete drivers.

(a) **Symmetry with the public URL.** CC-0088 landed `https://keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io/v3` as the documented public access path in the Quick Start, and the CRD's `status.endpoint` is derived as `https://{spec.gateway.hostname}/v3` — both consistently use the bare `keystone` label. The internal/admin rows in the catalog should match: there is no architectural reason for the internal hostname to carry an extra `-api` suffix when the public one does not, and the asymmetry shows up in every fresh Quick Start `openstack endpoint list` output.

(b) **The `-api` suffix is dead semantic weight.** It dates from a time when an operator might have managed multiple sibling components per CR (`keystone-api`, `keystone-something-else`), but in this codebase the Keystone operator only manages a single workload per CR. `Deployment/keystone`, `Service/keystone`, `HPA/keystone`, `PodDisruptionBudget/keystone`, `NetworkPolicy/keystone`, `HTTPRoute/keystone` — the kind disambiguates them perfectly, the `metadata.name` already matches the CR `metadata.name`, and a `kubectl describe keystone <name> && kubectl get all -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=<name>` walk reads cleanly without a parallel `<name>-api` family of objects sitting alongside.

(c) **`bootstrapServiceURL()` reimplements the naming convention instead of delegating to `apiResourceName()`.** That is a latent bug independent of the suffix question: any future change to the Service name pattern would silently desync the bootstrap-seeded catalog URL from the actual Service hostname. Folding this single hardcoded `fmt.Sprintf` into the central helper closes the divergence channel at the same time as the rename, with no extra cost.

This change is the natural follow-up to CC-0088 (#282) and lines the internal posture up with the public posture established by that feature.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_hpa.go:26-28` — flip `apiResourceName()` body to `return keystone.Name`; consider renaming the helper to a suffix-neutral identifier
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go:67-68` — refactor `bootstrapServiceURL()` to delegate to `apiResourceName(keystone)` so the bootstrap URL tracks the helper automatically
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:171,178` — rename container name and port name from `"keystone-api"` to `"keystone"`
- All controller-package tests that assert on resource names, container/port names, or cluster-internal URLs (the `*_test.go` files listed under "Concrete steps" #4 above)
- e2e fixtures listed under "Concrete steps" #5 above
- Documentation sites listed under "Concrete steps" #6 above (`docs/`, `architecture/docs/`, plus the `tests/unit/docs/quick_start_access_section_test.sh` gate that locks the Quick Start Fallback subsection wording)
- Doc-comment cleanups listed under "Concrete steps" #7 above

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `apiResourceName(keystone)` returns `keystone.Name` (no suffix) — verified by a focused unit test
- [ ] On a freshly bootstrapped cluster, `openstack endpoint list` returns `internal` and `admin` rows with URL `http://keystone.<ns>.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3` (no `-api`)
- [ ] `kubectl get svc -n openstack -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=<keystone-cr-name>` returns a single Service named `<keystone-cr-name>` (matching `metadata.name` of the CR), with no Service named `<keystone-cr-name>-api`
- [ ] `kubectl get deploy,hpa,pdb,networkpolicy,httproute -n openstack -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=<keystone-cr-name>` reports objects all named `<keystone-cr-name>`
- [ ] `bootstrapServiceURL()` no longer contains the literal string `"-api"`; its return value is computed by composing `apiResourceName(keystone)` with the namespace and the `:5000/v3` suffix
- [ ] `grep -rn 'keystone-api'` across `operators/`, `tests/`, `docs/`, `architecture/docs/`, `hack/`, `deploy/` returns zero hits — every site is either renamed to `keystone` or, where the literal must remain (legacy migration callouts in upgrade docs), explicitly annotated as a pre-rename artefact
- [ ] All controller-package unit tests pass against the renamed fixtures
- [ ] The chainsaw e2e suites under `tests/e2e/keystone/` and `tests/e2e-chaos/` pass on a kind cluster after the rename, including the PodChaos suite that selects API Pods by label
- [ ] The Quick Start `tests/unit/docs/quick_start_access_section_test.sh` gate still passes — the Fallback subsection now references `kubectl port-forward svc/keystone` and the primary section continues to omit any port-forward
- [ ] An upgrade callout is added to `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md` explaining the Service rename and the catalog self-heal path
- [ ] The CRD reference (`docs/reference/keystone-crd.md`) documents the new sub-resource naming convention (no `-api` suffix) and links to the upgrade callout
- [ ] Reconciliation of an existing pre-rename CR on the upgrade path is verified end-to-end: the new Deployment/Service/HPA come up with the new names, the old `<name>-api` objects are GC'd by ownerReference cascade (or explicitly deleted by a one-shot migration step in the controller — see Non-Goals), the BootstrapReady condition flips back to True, and `openstack endpoint list` reflects the new internal URL
- [ ] No selector or label changes are required: `selectorLabels()` and `commonLabels()` already key off `keystone.Name` only and are unaffected

**Non-Goals**:
- Adding a runtime migration step inside the controller to delete the old `<name>-api` Service/Deployment/HPA/PDB/NetworkPolicy/HTTPRoute objects on first reconcile after the upgrade. OwnerReference cascade should handle this when the operator stops creating the old-named objects, but it relies on the controller-runtime cache catching the orphan. If field testing shows orphans persist, follow up with a dedicated migration sub-reconciler — out of scope here.
- Renaming the CRD itself, the `Keystone` Go type, or the `keystone.c5c3.io` API group. The change is strictly to operator-managed sub-resource names, not the CR.
- Changing the public URL or the `spec.gateway.hostname` semantics. CC-0088 already settled the public posture; this issue only aligns the internal posture with it.
- Touching `keystone_authtoken` configuration in any other OpenStack service. The catalog self-heal via `keystone-manage bootstrap` covers Keystone itself; downstream services that hardcode `keystone-api.<ns>.svc.cluster.local` in their config are outside this operator's surface area and need their own updates by their respective operators or operators of operators.
- Backwards-compatibility shim: the operator will not create both `<name>` and `<name>-api` Services in parallel. The rename is a clean cut at one Generation boundary, documented in the upgrade flow.

**References**:
- CC-0088 / #282 / commit `88323b9` — the public-side rename to `keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io` that motivates internal/public symmetry
- `apiResourceName()` central helper: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_hpa.go:26-28`
- `bootstrapServiceURL()` hardcoded pattern: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go:67-68`
- Container/port name literals: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:171,178`
- Quick Start internal-URL examples: `docs/quick-start.md:638,699,736-740`

**Labels:** enhancement

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/285

stories.title: OpenStack operator sees symmetric internal/public Keystone hostnames in the catalog (CC-0095)
stories.want: the internal and admin endpoint rows in `openstack endpoint list` to use the bare `keystone.<ns>.svc.cluster.local` hostname
stories.so_that: the service catalog mirrors the public posture established by CC-0088 and there is no asymmetric `-api` suffix on the cluster-internal rows
criteria: After bootstrap, `openstack endpoint list` shows `internal` and `admin` rows with URL `http://keystone.<ns>.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3` (no `-api` segment)
The `public` row continues to use `https://<spec.gateway.hostname>/v3` (CC-0088 unchanged)
Re-rolling the bootstrap Job after a Generation change updates the catalog rows in place via `keystone-manage bootstrap` (CC-0095, REQ-005)
`status.endpoint` on the Keystone CR continues to reflect either the public Gateway URL or the renamed internal URL when no Gateway is configured

stories.title: Cluster operator inspects sub-resources with names that match the CR name (CC-0095)
stories.want: every operator-managed sub-resource (Deployment, Service, PDB, HPA, NetworkPolicy, HTTPRoute) to be named exactly after the Keystone CR `metadata.name`
stories.so_that: `kubectl get all -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=<cr-name>` reads cleanly and `kubectl describe <kind>/<cr-name>` resolves without a parallel `<cr-name>-api` family
criteria: `kubectl get svc -n <ns> -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=<cr-name>` returns a single Service named `<cr-name>` (CC-0095, REQ-004)
`kubectl get deploy,hpa,pdb,networkpolicy,httproute -n <ns> -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=<cr-name>` reports objects all named `<cr-name>` (CC-0095, REQ-004)

`kubectl get pod -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone,app.kubernetes.io/instance=<cr-name>` returns the API pods (selector unchanged because `selectorLabels()`/`commonLabels()` already key off `keystone.Name`)
After a CR Generation bump, no orphan `<cr-name>-api` Service/Deployment remains (ownerReference cascade deletes the old objects)

stories.title: Operator developer trusts that the bootstrap URL tracks the resource-name helper (CC-0095)
stories.want: `bootstrapServiceURL()` to delegate to `apiResourceName(keystone)` instead of re-implementing the naming pattern
stories.so_that: future changes to the sub-resource naming convention cannot silently desync the bootstrap-seeded catalog URL from the actual Service hostname
criteria: `bootstrapServiceURL()` contains no literal string `"-api"` and no `%s-api` format directive (CC-0095, REQ-002)
`bootstrapServiceURL()` composes its return value from `apiResourceName(keystone)`, the namespace, and the `:5000/v3` suffix
A unit test verifies that any future change to `apiResourceName()` is automatically reflected in `bootstrapServiceURL()` output (golden assertion that the two share a name segment)
The container and named port literals `"keystone-api"` in `reconcile_deployment.go` are renamed to `"keystone"` (CC-0095, REQ-003)

stories.title: Cluster operator upgrading the operator understands the rename and catalog self-heal (CC-0095)
stories.want: explicit upgrade-path documentation describing the Service rename, the implications for the catalog, and the self-heal mechanism
stories.so_that: I can plan the upgrade window, anticipate that any downstream service hard-coding `keystone-api.<ns>.svc.cluster.local` needs a config update, and decide between automatic re-bootstrap vs manual `openstack endpoint set`
criteria: `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md` includes a CC-0095 callout explaining the Service rename, why the catalog self-heals via `keystone-manage bootstrap`, and the two operator workflows: (a) trigger a Generation bump for automatic re-bootstrap, (b) manual `openstack endpoint set` against the two cluster-internal rows
`docs/reference/keystone-crd.md` documents the new sub-resource naming convention (no `-api` suffix) and links to the upgrade callout (CC-0095, REQ-008)
The callout names the affected sub-resources (Deployment, Service, PDB, HPA, NetworkPolicy, HTTPRoute) and notes that ownerReference cascade GCs the old `<name>-api` objects

The callout flags that downstream OpenStack services with hard-coded `keystone_authtoken.www_authenticate_uri` pointing at `keystone-api.<ns>.svc.cluster.local` are out of scope and must be updated by their respective operators

stories.title: Documentation reader follows examples that match the new internal hostname (CC-0095)
stories.want: every cluster-internal hostname example in `docs/`, `architecture/docs/`, and the Quick Start to use `keystone.openstack.svc.cluster.local`
stories.so_that: copy-paste commands from the docs work against a freshly bootstrapped cluster after the rename
criteria: `docs/quick-start.md` (lines 638, 699, 736-740) references `keystone.openstack.svc.cluster.local` and `svc/keystone` (no `-api`)

The Quick Start Fallback subsection uses `kubectl port-forward svc/keystone -n openstack 5000:5000` and the matching unit doc test in `tests/unit/docs/quick_start_access_section_test.sh` continues to pass after both are updated together (CC-0095, REQ-006)

`docs/guides/observability.md`, `docs/guides/advanced-configuration.md`, `docs/guides/day-2-operations.md`, `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md`, `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md`, `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md`, and `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md` carry no stray `keystone-api.<ns>.svc.cluster.local` strings outside annotated legacy callouts

Architecture docs (`architecture/docs/09-implementation/{04,06,10}*.md`, `architecture/docs/05-deployment/03-service-configuration/01-config-generation.md`, `architecture/docs/06-operations/02-observability/02-logging.md`) reference the renamed container `keystone` in code examples

stories.title: CI engineer observes green chainsaw e2e and chaos suites after the rename (CC-0095)
stories.want: every chainsaw e2e fixture and chaos-mesh PodChaos resource to assert against the new container/Service names
stories.so_that: the post-rename pipeline does not regress on container-name selectors, PDB resource names, or PodChaos label selectors
criteria: `tests/e2e/keystone/resources/chainsaw-test.yaml` (lines 49, 98) asserts container name `keystone`
`tests/e2e/keystone/uwsgi/chainsaw-test.yaml` (lines 49, 106) asserts container name `keystone`
`tests/e2e/keystone/graceful-shutdown/chainsaw-test.yaml:73` and `tests/e2e/keystone/configmap-no-secrets/chainsaw-test.yaml:166` reference container `keystone`

`tests/e2e-chaos/api-pod-kill-pdb/chainsaw-test.yaml` references PDB `keystone-chaos-api` (renamed from `keystone-chaos-api-api`) and Deployment `keystone-chaos-api` (renamed from `keystone-chaos-api-api`); the PodChaos label selector (`app.kubernetes.io/instance=keystone-chaos-api`) is unchanged because it targets the CR name, not the sub-resource name (CC-0095, REQ-004)
The chainsaw suite passes end-to-end on a kind cluster after the rename

stories.title: Future maintainer reads helper-comments that match the renamed code (CC-0095)
stories.want: the apologetic doc-comments referring to the `keystone-api container` to be cleaned up to refer to the bare `keystone` container
stories.so_that: future readers of `reconcile_policyvalidation.go`, `reconcile_fernet.go`, `reconcile_credential.go`, `reconcile_database.go`, and `reconcile_bootstrap.go` are not confused by stale framing
criteria: Comments in `reconcile_policyvalidation.go:168`, `reconcile_fernet.go:317`, `reconcile_credential.go:322`, `reconcile_database.go:620`, and `reconcile_bootstrap.go:134` no longer reference `keystone-api container` (CC-0095, REQ-009)
Comments referring to `containerResources()` continue to point at the same helper, with the renamed container framing
All updated comments include the CC-0095 Feature ID for traceability
No comment introduces a forward-looking TODO or `// removed for CC-0095` artefact — comments are simply rewritten in place

requirements.description: The `apiResourceName(keystone)` helper SHALL return `keystone.Name` directly with no suffix transformation (CC-0095)
requirements.rationale: Centralises the naming convention in one location so the suffix removal is a single-line change that all sub-resource builders inherit

scenarios.name: Helper returns bare CR name
scenarios.when: `apiResourceName(keystone)` is called with a Keystone CR named `test-keystone`
scenarios.then: the helper returns `test-keystone` (no `-api` suffix)
and_then: Adding a new builder that calls the helper inherits the suffix-free naming automatically

scenarios.name: Helper preserves CR names that contain `-api` substring
scenarios.when: `apiResourceName(keystone)` is called with a CR named `keystone-chaos-api` (the e2e-chaos fixture)
scenarios.then: the helper returns `keystone-chaos-api` verbatim (the suffix is not stripped from the CR name itself, only from the helper output)
and_then: The chaos-suite Deployment is named `keystone-chaos-api` (one segment, not `keystone-chaos-api-api`)

scenarios.name: Helper returns empty string only for empty CR name
scenarios.when: `apiResourceName(keystone)` is called with `keystone.Name == ""`
scenarios.then: the helper returns the empty string (no synthetic `-api` artefact remains)
and_then: Empty-name input is the responsibility of CRD validation, not this helper

requirements.description: `bootstrapServiceURL(keystone)` SHALL compose its return value from `apiResourceName(keystone)`, the CR namespace, and the `:5000/v3` suffix without any literal `-api` string (CC-0095)
requirements.rationale: Eliminates the latent divergence channel where the bootstrap-seeded catalog URL could desync from the actual Service hostname; collapses two sources of truth into one

scenarios.name: Bootstrap URL matches Service DNS
scenarios.when: `bootstrapServiceURL(keystone)` is called with a CR named `keystone` in namespace `openstack`
scenarios.then: the function returns `http://keystone.openstack.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3`
and_then: The same string equals the cluster DNS name of the Service named by `apiResourceName(keystone)` in the same namespace

scenarios.name: Bootstrap URL contains no `-api` literal
scenarios.when: the source code of `bootstrapServiceURL()` is inspected after the refactor
scenarios.then: the function body contains no literal string `"-api"` and no `%s-api` format directive
and_then: A future change to `apiResourceName()` automatically propagates to the bootstrap URL

scenarios.name: Bootstrap URL tracks helper changes
scenarios.when: a hypothetical future patch changes `apiResourceName()` to prefix or suffix the CR name
scenarios.then: the next call to `bootstrapServiceURL()` reflects that change verbatim in the URL host segment
and_then: A regression test asserts that `apiResourceName(keystone)` is a substring of the URL returned by `bootstrapServiceURL(keystone)`

requirements.description: The Deployment template SHALL use container name `keystone` and named container port `keystone` (renamed from `keystone-api`) (CC-0095)
requirements.rationale: Container/port names are the only two literal `keystone-api` strings inside the Deployment template; renaming them mirrors the resource-name change and keeps `kubectl logs <pod> -c keystone` symmetric with `Service/keystone`

scenarios.name: Container is named `keystone`
scenarios.when: `buildKeystoneDeployment()` is invoked
scenarios.then: the resulting Deployment has exactly one container with `Name == "keystone"`
and_then: `kubectl logs <pod> -c keystone` resolves the container without falling back to `keystone-api`

scenarios.name: Named port is `keystone`
scenarios.when: `buildKeystoneDeployment()` is invoked
scenarios.then: the container's port array contains exactly one entry with `Name == "keystone"` and `ContainerPort == 5000`
and_then: Service `targetPort` continues to use the int `5000` (not the named port), so no Service update is needed
HTTPRoute `backendRef.port` continues to use the int `5000`, so no HTTPRoute update is needed

scenarios.name: No `keystone-api` literal remains in the Deployment template
scenarios.when: `reconcile_deployment.go` is grep'd for `keystone-api`
scenarios.then: the search returns zero hits inside `buildKeystoneDeployment()`
and_then: The only remaining hits in the package are inside doc-comments (cleaned up by REQ-009)

requirements.description: Every operator-managed sub-resource (Deployment, Service, PodDisruptionBudget, HorizontalPodAutoscaler, NetworkPolicy, HTTPRoute) SHALL be named `<cr.metadata.name>` with no `-api` suffix (CC-0095)
requirements.rationale: Aligns sub-resource names with the CR name so a `kubectl get all -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=<cr-name>` walk reads cleanly without parallel `<name>-api` siblings; matches the public-posture symmetry established by CC-0088

scenarios.name: Deployment, Service, PDB, HPA all named after the CR
scenarios.when: the operator reconciles a CR named `keystone` in namespace `openstack`
scenarios.then: Deployment/keystone, Service/keystone, PDB/keystone, HPA/keystone exist (when their respective spec fields enable them)
and_then: No `keystone-api` Service, Deployment, PDB, or HPA is created

scenarios.name: NetworkPolicy and HTTPRoute also follow the convention
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is set OR spec.gateway is set
scenarios.then: the resulting NetworkPolicy and HTTPRoute carry `metadata.name == cr.metadata.name`
and_then: The HTTPRoute `backendRef.name` references the renamed Service (also `cr.metadata.name`)

scenarios.name: Selector and label invariants are unchanged
scenarios.when: the renamed sub-resources are listed by label
scenarios.then: `selectorLabels(keystone)` and `commonLabels(keystone)` continue to key off `keystone.Name` only, with no schema change
and_then: Existing Pods continue to be selected by the renamed Service/PDB/NetworkPolicy without label drift

requirements.description: On a freshly bootstrapped cluster, the Keystone service catalog SHALL contain `internal` and `admin` endpoint rows with URL `http://<cr.name>.<ns>.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3` (CC-0095)
requirements.rationale: Closes the loop on REQ-002: not only does the source code compose the URL via the helper, but the actual catalog content visible to `openstack endpoint list` reflects the rename

scenarios.name: Fresh bootstrap writes the renamed URL
scenarios.when: a new Keystone CR is reconciled and the bootstrap Job completes
scenarios.then: `openstack endpoint list` shows the `internal` and `admin` rows pointing at `http://<cr.name>.<ns>.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3`
and_then: The `public` row continues to use `https://<spec.gateway.hostname>/v3` per CC-0088

scenarios.name: Re-bootstrap on Generation change updates existing catalog rows
scenarios.when: an existing CR with the old `<name>-api` URL in the catalog is upgraded and Generation bumps
scenarios.then: `BootstrapReady` flips to False, the bootstrap Job re-runs, and the catalog rows are updated in-place to the new URL
and_then: No manual `openstack endpoint set` is required for Keystone's own catalog entries

scenarios.name: PublicEndpoint precedence is preserved
scenarios.when: spec.bootstrap.publicEndpoint is set non-empty
scenarios.then: the `public` row uses publicEndpoint verbatim while `internal` and `admin` use the renamed cluster-local URL
and_then: The bootstrap script continues to seed all three rows in a single `keystone-manage bootstrap` invocation

requirements.description: The doc-test gate `tests/unit/docs/quick_start_access_section_test.sh` SHALL continue to pass after both the Quick Start Fallback subsection and the test assertions are updated to reference `keystone` instead of `keystone-api` (CC-0095)
requirements.rationale: The Fallback subsection is locked by an automated assertion that searches for the literal `kubectl port-forward svc/keystone-api`; updating one without the other would break the gate

scenarios.name: Primary section continues to omit port-forward
scenarios.when: the test runs against the updated Quick Start
scenarios.then: the primary `## Access Keystone from your local machine` section contains no `kubectl port-forward svc/keystone` command
and_then: The nip.io explainer paragraph remains in the primary section

scenarios.name: Fallback subsection contains the renamed port-forward
scenarios.when: the test runs against the updated Quick Start
scenarios.then: the `### Fallback — kubectl port-forward` subsection contains `kubectl port-forward svc/keystone -n openstack 5000:5000`
and_then: Test 5's grep target is updated from `kubectl port-forward svc/keystone-api` to `kubectl port-forward svc/keystone`

scenarios.name: Test gate is updated atomically with the doc
scenarios.when: either the doc OR the test is updated in isolation
scenarios.then: the test fails (catching half-applied changes during PR review)
and_then: Both files are updated in the same task to keep the gate green

requirements.description: The upgrade-flow reference SHALL include a CC-0095 callout describing the sub-resource rename, the catalog self-heal mechanism, and the operator's two re-bootstrap workflows (CC-0095)
requirements.rationale: Operators upgrading the operator from a pre-CC-0095 version need to know that the rename happens at one Generation boundary, that ownerReference cascade GCs the old objects, and that `keystone-manage bootstrap` re-seeds the catalog in-place

scenarios.name: Callout names affected sub-resources
scenarios.when: a reader navigates `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md`
scenarios.then: the CC-0095 callout enumerates the renamed sub-resources (Deployment, Service, PDB, HPA, NetworkPolicy, HTTPRoute) and notes that the rename is a clean cut at one Generation boundary
and_then: No backwards-compatibility shim is documented; the operator does not create `<name>` and `<name>-api` in parallel

scenarios.name: Callout describes the catalog self-heal
scenarios.when: an operator reads the upgrade flow
scenarios.then: the doc explains that `keystone-manage bootstrap` is idempotent and updates the existing internal/admin rows in-place
and_then: Two operator workflows are listed: (a) Generation bump → automatic re-bootstrap, (b) manual `openstack endpoint set` against the two cluster-internal rows

scenarios.name: Callout flags downstream out-of-scope services
scenarios.when: an operator reads the upgrade flow
scenarios.then: the doc explicitly notes that downstream OpenStack services that hard-code `keystone_authtoken.www_authenticate_uri` pointing at `keystone-api.<ns>.svc.cluster.local` are out of scope and must be updated by their respective operators
and_then: The doc references CC-0088 for the prior public-side rename context

requirements.description: The CRD reference SHALL document that the operator-managed Service is now named after the CR `metadata.name` without suffix and link to the upgrade callout (CC-0095)
requirements.rationale: The CRD reference is the canonical place readers look for the relationship between CR `metadata.name` and sub-resource names; the rename invalidates any prior `<name>-api` text

scenarios.name: Naming convention paragraph added
scenarios.when: a reader navigates `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md`
scenarios.then: a CC-0095 paragraph documents that all operator-managed sub-resources (Deployment, Service, PDB, HPA, NetworkPolicy, HTTPRoute) are named `<cr.metadata.name>` with no suffix
and_then: The paragraph links to the upgrade callout in `keystone-upgrade-flow.md` for migration context

scenarios.name: Existing `keystone-api` references are removed
scenarios.when: the CRD reference is grep'd for `keystone-api`
scenarios.then: no remaining references exist outside annotated legacy callouts
and_then: Any pre-rename example URL is updated to `keystone.<ns>.svc.cluster.local`

scenarios.name: Cross-reference to selector labels is preserved
scenarios.when: a reader inspects how the CR name flows into sub-resource selection
scenarios.then: the doc notes that `selectorLabels()` and `commonLabels()` already key off `keystone.Name` and are unaffected by the rename
and_then: Label-based selectors used by chaos-mesh / monitoring continue to work unchanged

requirements.description: After the rename, `grep -rn 'keystone-api'` across `operators/`, `tests/`, `docs/`, `architecture/docs/`, `hack/`, and `deploy/` SHALL return zero hits except for explicitly-annotated legacy callouts in upgrade documentation (CC-0095)
requirements.rationale: The acceptance bar is `zero residual `keystone-api` strings` outside annotated legacy callouts; this prevents partially-applied renames from shipping

scenarios.name: All controller-package literals are renamed
scenarios.when: `grep -rn 'keystone-api' operators/keystone/` runs after the rename
scenarios.then: the only remaining hits are in legacy upgrade-callout strings explicitly annotated as pre-rename artefacts
and_then: Doc-comments in `reconcile_policyvalidation.go`, `reconcile_fernet.go`, `reconcile_credential.go`, `reconcile_database.go`, `reconcile_bootstrap.go` no longer reference `keystone-api container`

scenarios.name: All test fixtures and assertions are renamed
scenarios.when: `grep -rn 'keystone-api' tests/` runs after the rename
scenarios.then: no residual `*-api` container assertions, PDB names, or hostname strings remain (excluding the explicitly-annotated pre-rename callout in upgrade docs, if surfaced via tests)
and_then: The chainsaw e2e suites pass on a kind cluster

scenarios.name: All docs and architecture references are renamed
scenarios.when: `grep -rn 'keystone-api' docs/ architecture/docs/` runs after the rename
scenarios.then: the only remaining hits are in the CC-0095 upgrade callout (where the old name is quoted as a pre-rename artefact for migration context)
and_then: The `hack/deploy-infra.sh:936` comment about `keystone-api spec.gateway` is updated or removed for clarity

tasks.title: Flip apiResourceName() in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_hpa.go:26-28 to return keystone.Name directly; update the helper's doc-comment to drop the `"{name}-api"` framing and add a CC-0095 reference. Add unit tests TestApiResourceName_ReturnsCRNameWithoutSuffix and TestApiResourceName_EmptyCRName in reconcile_hpa_test.go (REQ-001).

Refactor bootstrapServiceURL() in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go:67-68 to compose `http://%s.%s.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3` with `apiResourceName(keystone), keystone.Namespace`. Update the doc-comment to note that the URL tracks the helper. Add unit tests TestBootstrapServiceURL_ComposesViaHelper and TestBootstrapServiceURL_NoApiLiteral (REQ-002).

Rename the container Name and named ContainerPort Name from `"keystone-api"` to `"keystone"` at operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:171,178. Add unit tests TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_ContainerNameIsKeystone and TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_NamedPortIsKeystone (REQ-003).

Touch up the doc-comments referring to `the keystone-api container` in reconcile_policyvalidation.go:168, reconcile_fernet.go:317, reconcile_credential.go:322, reconcile_database.go:620, and reconcile_bootstrap.go:134 — rewrite to reference `the keystone container` and add a CC-0095 trailing reference. No code changes (REQ-009).

Update the doc-comment for keystoneAPIPort const at reconcile_httproute.go:100-103 — drop the `"named '{name}-api'"` framing in the comment to reference the bare CR-name Service. No code change to the const value (REQ-009).

Update reconcile_deployment_test.go assertions (~21 sites): rewrite all `test-keystone-api` resource-name assertions to `test-keystone`, container `Name` assertions from `keystone-api` to `keystone`, port `Name` assertions from `keystone-api` to `keystone`. Add TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_NameMatchesCR, TestBuildKeystoneService_NameMatchesCR, TestBuildPodDisruptionBudget_NameMatchesCR (REQ-003, REQ-004).
Update reconcile_hpa_test.go assertions (~10 sites): rewrite all `test-keystone-api` HPA-name and ScaleTargetRef.Name assertions to `test-keystone`. Add TestBuildKeystoneHPA_NameMatchesCR (REQ-004).

Update reconcile_httproute_test.go assertions (~17 sites for `keystone-api`, ~17 sites for `test-keystone-api`): rewrite HTTPRoute name, BackendRef.Name, and internalAPIURL host-segment assertions. Add TestBuildKeystoneHTTPRoute_NameAndBackendRefMatchCR and TestInternalAPIURL_UsesBareCRName (REQ-004, REQ-005).

Update reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go assertions (~9 sites): rewrite NetworkPolicy-name assertions from `test-keystone-api` to `test-keystone`. Add TestBuildKeystoneNetworkPolicy_NameMatchesCR (REQ-004).

Update reconcile_healthcheck_test.go assertions (~3 sites): rewrite the cluster-internal Keystone API URL fixtures from `http://test-keystone-api.<ns>...` to `http://test-keystone.<ns>...` and any `keystone-api` container-name reference (REQ-005).

Update reconcile_bootstrap_test.go assertions: rewrite expected bootstrap script substrings (`--bootstrap-internal-url`, `--bootstrap-admin-url`) to use bare-name URLs. Add TestBuildBootstrapJob_AdminInternalURLsUseBareName covering the script content (REQ-005).
Update keystone_controller_test.go assertions (single `keystone-api` reference per grep — confirm and rewrite). Verify no other CR-name + `-api` literal remains in this file (REQ-004).

Update integration_test.go assertions (~32 sites for `keystone-api`, ~24 for `test-keystone-api`): rewrite all sub-resource Name lookups (Deployment, Service, PDB, HPA, NetworkPolicy, HTTPRoute) to bare CR name; rewrite container/port-name expectations. Add TestIntegration_ReconcileProducesRenamedSubResources and TestIntegration_NoOrphanedApiSuffixedResourcesAfterReconcile (REQ-004).

Run `go test ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/...` locally and fix any cascade failures from helper-method renames or missing assertions. Confirm zero `keystone-api` literal in the controller package outside annotated legacy comments (REQ-009).
Update tests/e2e/keystone/resources/chainsaw-test.yaml container-name assertions at lines 49 and 98 from `keystone-api` to `keystone` (REQ-003).
Update tests/e2e/keystone/uwsgi/chainsaw-test.yaml container-name assertions at lines 49 and 106 from `keystone-api` to `keystone` (REQ-003).
Update tests/e2e/keystone/graceful-shutdown/chainsaw-test.yaml line 73 container reference from `keystone-api` to `keystone` (REQ-003).
Update tests/e2e/keystone/configmap-no-secrets/chainsaw-test.yaml line 166 container reference from `keystone-api` to `keystone` (REQ-003).

Update tests/e2e-chaos/api-pod-kill-pdb/chainsaw-test.yaml: rewrite PDB Name assertion at :54,60 from `keystone-chaos-api-api` to `keystone-chaos-api`; rewrite Deployment-name lookups at :81,93 from `keystone-chaos-api-api` to `keystone-chaos-api`. Verify the PodChaos label selector (`app.kubernetes.io/instance=keystone-chaos-api`) at 01-podchaos.yaml is unchanged (it targets the CR name, not the sub-resource name). Optionally rename PodChaos `metadata.name: kill-keystone-api` for cosmetic clarity (REQ-004).

Update docs/quick-start.md cluster-internal URL examples: line 638 (`http://keystone-api.openstack.svc:5000/v3` → `http://keystone.openstack.svc:5000/v3`), line 699 (catalog Note URL), and the Fallback subsection at lines 736-740 (`svc/keystone-api` → `svc/keystone`, prose mention `port-forwarding the keystone-api Service` → `port-forwarding the keystone Service`) (REQ-005, REQ-006).

Update tests/unit/docs/quick_start_access_section_test.sh: change the search targets in test_no_portforward_before_fallback (line 80) and test_fallback_contains_portforward (line 136) from `kubectl port-forward svc/keystone-api` to `kubectl port-forward svc/keystone`. Update the file's header comment to add a CC-0095 reference (REQ-006).

Update docs/reference/keystone-crd.md: rewrite the existing 2 `keystone-api` references and add a CC-0095 paragraph describing the new sub-resource naming convention (no `-api` suffix). Link to the upgrade-flow callout. Add tests/unit/docs/keystone_crd_naming_convention_test.sh that asserts the paragraph exists (REQ-008).
Update docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md: rewrite the 11 `keystone-api` references (Service-name, container-name, hostname examples) to use the bare `keystone` form (REQ-009).

Update docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md: add a CC-0095 callout (e.g. `## Sub-Resource Rename (CC-0095)`) describing the rename, listing affected sub-resources, explaining the `keystone-manage bootstrap` catalog self-heal, and documenting the two operator workflows (Generation bump vs manual `openstack endpoint set`). Cross-link to the CRD reference. Rewrite the existing 1 `keystone-api` reference, retaining old-name quoting only inside the legacy callout (annotated as a pre-rename artefact). Add tests/unit/docs/keystone_upgrade_flow_cc0095_callout_test.sh that asserts the callout exists (REQ-007).
Update docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md: rewrite the 2 `keystone-api` references (PDB-name and selector examples) (REQ-009).
Update docs/guides/observability.md: rewrite the 2 `keystone-api` references (container-name and Service-DNS examples) (REQ-009).
Update docs/guides/advanced-configuration.md: rewrite the 2 `keystone-api` references to bare `keystone` form (REQ-009).
Update docs/guides/day-2-operations.md: rewrite the 1 `keystone-api` reference to bare `keystone` form (REQ-009).

Update architecture docs (read-only-by-default but explicitly listed in the feature description): rewrite `keystone-api` references in architecture/docs/09-implementation/04-keystone-reconciler.md (2 sites), 06-testing.md (2 sites), 10-chaos-e2e-testing.md (2 sites), architecture/docs/05-deployment/03-service-configuration/01-config-generation.md (5 sites), architecture/docs/06-operations/02-observability/02-logging.md (1 site). Confirm with the feature owner before editing if architecture is locked in this repo (REQ-009).
Update or remove the comment in hack/deploy-infra.sh:936 referring to `keystone-api spec.gateway` — rewrite to `Keystone CR's spec.gateway` for clarity. No script-behavior change (REQ-009).

Add scripts/grep-keystone-api-residue.sh — a CI-runnable shell script that runs `grep -rn 'keystone-api' operators/ tests/ docs/ architecture/docs/ hack/ deploy/` and exits non-zero if any hit appears outside lines marked with the `<!-- CC-0095 legacy -->` annotation. Run it locally and confirm exit 0 (REQ-009).

Run `make test` (controller-package envtest + unit tests) and confirm green. Run `bash tests/unit/docs/quick_start_access_section_test.sh` and the new doc-test gates and confirm green. Document any envtest setup quirks encountered.

Run the chainsaw e2e suite (`tests/e2e/keystone/` and `tests/e2e-chaos/`) on a kind cluster (or report sandbox limitation if cluster is unavailable per the user's project memory). Confirm: PodChaos label selector continues to match API pods; PDB minAvailable assertion passes against the renamed PDB; container-name assertions in resources/uwsgi/graceful-shutdown/configmap-no-secrets pass.

Manual upgrade-path verification on a kind cluster: deploy a pre-CC-0095 Keystone CR (with old `<name>-api` sub-resources), upgrade the operator, bump the CR Generation, observe that (a) new `<name>` sub-resources come up, (b) old `<name>-api` sub-resources are GC'd by ownerReference cascade, (c) BootstrapReady flips back to True, (d) `openstack endpoint list` reflects the new internal URL. Document any observed orphans for follow-up (Non-Goal: dedicated migration sub-reconciler).

review_criteria: [blocker] `apiResourceName()` returns `keystone.Name` directly with no fmt.Sprintf or suffix; verified by TestApiResourceName_ReturnsCRNameWithoutSuffix (REQ-001)
[blocker] `bootstrapServiceURL()` body contains no literal `"-api"` string and no `%s-api` format directive; verified by TestBootstrapServiceURL_NoApiLiteral (REQ-002)
[blocker] Container `Name` and named ContainerPort `Name` in `buildKeystoneDeployment()` are `"keystone"` — NOT `"keystone-api"` (REQ-003)

[blocker] Every operator-managed sub-resource (Deployment, Service, PDB, HPA, NetworkPolicy, HTTPRoute) is named exactly `keystone.Name` — verified by integration test that lists all owned objects and asserts no `<name>-api` survivor (REQ-004)

[blocker] `tests/unit/docs/quick_start_access_section_test.sh` passes after both the doc and the test are updated together; the test's grep targets are updated from `keystone-api` to `keystone` (REQ-006)

[blocker] `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md` contains a CC-0095 callout describing the sub-resource rename, the catalog self-heal mechanism, and the two operator workflows — verified by tests/unit/docs/keystone_upgrade_flow_cc0095_callout_test.sh (REQ-007)
[blocker] `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md` documents the new sub-resource naming convention and links to the upgrade callout (REQ-008)

[blocker] `grep -rn 'keystone-api' operators/ tests/ docs/ architecture/docs/ hack/ deploy/` returns only annotated CC-0095 legacy callouts; CI gate `scripts/grep-keystone-api-residue.sh` exits 0 (REQ-009)
[critical] `selectorLabels()` and `commonLabels()` are NOT modified — verified by inspecting the diff (label schema must remain stable so existing chaos-mesh / monitoring selectors continue to work)

[critical] HTTPRoute `BackendRef.Port` and Service `TargetPort` continue to use the int `5000` (not the named port) — the named port rename in REQ-003 is local-cosmetic and must not propagate cross-resource changes

[critical] PodChaos label selector at `tests/e2e-chaos/api-pod-kill-pdb/01-podchaos.yaml` is unchanged (selector targets `app.kubernetes.io/instance=keystone-chaos-api`, the CR name) — only PDB-name and Deployment-name lookups in `chainsaw-test.yaml` are updated
[critical] All test assertions are updated atomically with the source rename — no test left asserting on the old `*-api` literal (avoids the partial-rename PR pattern)
[critical] All Feature ID references in new code/comments/docs use `CC-0095` per the feature-id-required rule
[minor] Doc-comments touched by Task 1.4 use a uniform tone (drop `keystone-api container` framing across all 5 sites) — no half-updated comment remains

[minor] If `apiResourceName()` is renamed to `coreResourceName()` or `subResourceName()`, the rename is mechanical (search-and-replace within the package) and does not change function semantics — preserve the option but do not require it

[minor] Architecture docs are updated only if confirmed in scope; if the project policy treats `architecture/` as read-only, document the divergence in implementation_notes and leave those sites for a follow-up
implementation_notes:
**Architecture & data flow (CC-0095 rename refactor)**

```
     Keystone CR (metadata.name = N)
              │
              ▼
     apiResourceName(cr) ──────────►  N           ◄── single source of truth
              │                       (was: N-api)
              │
   ┌──────────┼──────────┬────────────┬───────────┬─────────────┐
   ▼          ▼          ▼            ▼           ▼             ▼
Deployment  Service     PDB         HPA      NetworkPolicy   HTTPRoute
  /N         /N         /N          /N          /N             /N
  │                                                              │
  │  container.Name == 'keystone'                                │
  │  port.Name      == 'keystone'                                │
  │  port           == 5000 (int)                                │
  │                                                              │
  └─► buildBootstrapJob ──► keystone-manage bootstrap ──► catalog rows:
        --internal-url     internal: http://N.<ns>.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3
        --admin-url        admin:    http://N.<ns>.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3
        --public-url       public:   https://<spec.gateway.hostname>/v3
              ▲
              │ (composes via apiResourceName(cr) — REQ-002)
     bootstrapServiceURL(cr)
```

**Architectural decisions**
1. **One source of truth**: `apiResourceName()` becomes the single naming function. `bootstrapServiceURL()` MUST delegate to it (REQ-002) — this closes the latent divergence channel that the issue calls out as driver (c). Future renames touch one helper, not many call sites.
2. **Local-cosmetic container/port rename**: The container `Name` and named-port `Name` literals at `reconcile_deployment.go:171,178` are LOCAL to the Deployment template. Service `TargetPort` and HTTPRoute `BackendRef.Port` use ints, not names — verified at `reconcile_deployment.go:461` and `reconcile_httproute.go:236-238`. The rename therefore does not propagate to those resources. This is the smallest possible blast radius for the cosmetic rename and is the right shape.
3. **Selector/label invariants are preserved**: `selectorLabels()` and `commonLabels()` already key off `keystone.Name` only (verified via grep). They are NOT touched. This means the rename is invisible to label-based consumers (chaos-mesh PodChaos, Prometheus ServiceMonitors, NetworkPolicy peer-PodSelectors).
4. **Catalog self-heal via `keystone-manage bootstrap`**: This is the migration mechanism. The bootstrap reconciler runs the script idempotently on every reconcile pass where BootstrapReady=False, and `keystone-manage bootstrap` updates existing endpoint rows in-place by region+interface+service. After Generation bumps, BootstrapReady flips False → the script re-runs → catalog self-heals. NO controller migration code is needed (this is documented as a Non-Goal).
5. **OwnerReference cascade handles orphan cleanup**: The operator owns its sub-resources via `controllerutil.SetControllerReference` (set in `deployment.EnsureXxx` helpers under `internal/common/deployment`). When the operator stops creating `<name>-api` and starts creating `<name>`, the kube-controller-manager's GC will reap the orphaned `<name>-api` sub-resources via ownerReference cascade. This is the default Kubernetes behavior and matches the design choice in the Non-Goals section. Field testing in Task 5.4 verifies that no orphans persist; a fallback migration sub-reconciler is explicitly out of scope.
6. **Test-tier coverage**: The project has unit tests (`*_test.go` in controller package), integration tests (`integration_test.go`, envtest-driven), e2e tests (`tests/e2e/keystone/`), and chaos-e2e tests (`tests/e2e-chaos/`), plus shell-based doc-tests (`tests/unit/docs/`). The plan covers ALL FIVE tiers — Phase 2 covers unit and integration test updates; Phase 3 covers e2e and chaos-e2e fixture updates; Phase 4 covers the doc-test gates and adds two new doc-tests for the CRD reference and upgrade-flow callout; Phase 5 covers end-to-end verification on a kind cluster.

**Sequencing rationale**
- Phase 1 (source rename) MUST land before Phase 2 (test updates) — the unit tests are written/updated to match the renamed source code, so they would fail against the old source. Tests in Phase 1 (TestApiResourceName_*, TestBootstrapServiceURL_*, TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_*) are written together with the source change in the same task, RED→GREEN style.
- Phase 2 (controller-package tests) MUST land before Phase 3 (e2e fixtures) — the e2e suites depend on the operator binary built from Phase 1+2 code. e2e fixtures with old `*-api` assertions would fail against the new operator binary.
- Phase 4 (docs) is ordered after Phase 1-3 because the doc updates describe the renamed behavior. The doc-test gate (Task 4.2) MUST update both the Quick Start AND the test in the same task to keep `quick_start_access_section_test.sh` green — REQ-006 codifies this constraint.
- Phase 5 (verification) runs after all source/test/doc changes are in place. The kind-cluster verification in Task 5.4 cannot run in the sandbox (per user's project memory `project_sandbox_no_docker.md`); the agent will report the limitation honestly and defer that step to a real environment.

**Pitfalls to avoid**
- DO NOT modify `selectorLabels()` or `commonLabels()` — they are explicitly out of scope and changing them would break label-based selectors across the cluster.
- DO NOT rename the CRD itself, the `Keystone` Go type, or the `keystone.openstack.c5c3.io` API group (Non-Goal).
- DO NOT introduce a backwards-compatibility shim that creates both `<name>` and `<name>-api` Services in parallel (Non-Goal — clean cut at one Generation boundary).
- DO NOT change the container `image` or any spec field beyond the literal name strings — only the Name fields at reconcile_deployment.go:171,178 are touched.
- WATCH OUT for CR fixture names that themselves contain `-api` (e.g. `keystone-chaos-api` in tests/e2e-chaos): the CR name must NOT be renamed. Only the sub-resource suffix is dropped, so the chaos-suite Deployment becomes `keystone-chaos-api` (one segment), not `keystone-chaos-api-api` (two segments).
- BE CAREFUL with the architecture/ docs: the planner instructions list `architecture/ (read-only reference)` while the feature description explicitly asks to update them. Task 4.10 calls this out and asks to confirm with the feature owner before editing if architecture is locked in this repo.
- Test names like `test-keystone` (controller-package fixture in `deployTestKeystone()` at reconcile_deployment_test.go:44-79) result in expected resource names `test-keystone` after the rename (was: `test-keystone-api`). Many test files have ~80-100 assertion sites that must be updated mechanically — Task 2.x estimates account for this volume.

**Patterns to follow (from the codebase)**
- Resource-name helper centralisation: `apiResourceName()` is the existing pattern; the rename strengthens it by adding `bootstrapServiceURL()` as a delegating consumer (REQ-002).
- Subreconciler naming: each `reconcile_<thing>.go` file owns its sub-resource and consumes `apiResourceName()` for its `metadata.Name` and any cross-resource references (e.g. HPA's `ScaleTargetRef.Name`, HTTPRoute's `BackendRef.Name`). This pattern is preserved unchanged.
- Label invariants: `selectorLabels()` and `commonLabels()` at `reconcile_deployment.go:135-142` use `keystonev1alpha1.LabelKeyName` and `LabelKeyInstance`, both keyed off `keystone.Name`. They are NOT touched.
- Doc-test gate pattern: `tests/unit/docs/*_test.sh` files lock specific doc subsections via shell asserts. The new doc-tests (Tasks 4.3, 4.5) follow this exact pattern.
